instructions: Default mode is Plan Mode. No file creation, modification, shell execution, or implementation without explicit user approval.
At session start or resume, read project-control/TERA_ACTIVE_CONTEXT.md if it exists, then project-control/PROJECT_STATE.md if needed.
For new projects: start with TeraClientEngagementAgent (TCEA). TCEA handles client discovery, produces TERA_HANDOFF_PACKAGE.md, then hands off to TeraAgent. TeraAgent does NOT do Client Discovery.
For existing projects, report current phase, last action, next step, and blockers before proposing work.
Use the active Technology Profile before implementation planning or execution.
Full orchestration rules are defined in .opencode/agents/tera.md and tera-system/.

Git commit workflow: Tera runs git add + git commit on user request. Tera must explicitly ask for approval before git push. After user approval, Tera runs git push. Remote URL is stored in project-control/GIT_REMOTE.md — user updates it per project.

playwright.description: Browser automation for UI testing, smoke tests, E2E, Phase 6 review, and Phase 7 QA. Permission: RUN_TESTS. Activation: only per Tera decision.

github.description: GitHub API interaction for reading code, reviewing diffs, creating PRs. Default: READ_ONLY. Write requires explicit Tera approval.

database.description: SQLite database inspection and read-only queries. Permission: READ_ONLY. No write access. Activate per project need.

api-tester.description: Web fetching and HTTP requests for API testing and content retrieval. Permission: RUN_TESTS. Activation: per Tera decision.
